title: SINTER
description: Returns the intersect of multiple sets.
complexity: O(N*M) worst case where N is the cardinality of the smallest set and M is the number of sets.

returns.description: A set with members of the resulting set
Long count of members of the resulting set.

nredisstack_async.signature: SetCombine(SetOperation operation, RedisKey[] keys, CommandFlags flags = CommandFlags.None)

params.description: keys pointing to sets to intersect
params.name: *keys

content:
Returns the members of the set resulting from the intersection of all the given sets. For example: ``` key1 = {a,b,c,d} key2 = {c} key3 = {a,c,e} SINTER key1 key2 key3 = {c} ``` Keys that do not exist are considered to be empty sets. With one of the keys being an empty set, the resulting set is also empty (since set intersection with an empty set always results in an empty set). ## Examples {{% redis-cli %}} SADD key1 "a" SADD key1 "b" SADD key1 "c" SADD key2 "c" SADD key2 "d" SADD key2 "e" SINTER key1 key2 {{% /redis-cli %}} ## Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility | Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | | ## Return information {{< multitabs id="sinter-return-info" tab1="RESP2" tab2="RESP3" >}} [Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays): an array with the members of the resulting set. -tab-sep- [Set reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#sets): a set with the members of the resulting set. {{< /multitabs >}}
example:
SADD key1 "a"
SADD key1 "b"
SADD key1 "c"
SADD key2 "c"
SADD key2 "d"
SADD key2 "e"
SINTER key1 key2
notes: This command's behavior varies in clustered Redis environments. See the [multi-key operations]() page for more information.

sections.title: Overview
Examples
sections.content:
{{% redis-cli %}}
SADD key1 "a"
SADD key1 "b"
SADD key1 "c"
SADD key2 "c"
SADD key2 "d"
SADD key2 "e"
SINTER key1 key2
{{% /redis-cli %}}
